2023-11-01.title: The fate of many Gazans lies with the Rafah crossing, and Egypt.

2023-11-01.title: How Germany’s Greens Lost Their Luster
2023-11-01.abstract: The party was riding high when it entered the government two years ago. Now it is stumbling, blamed for driving voters to the far right.

2023-11-01.title: Tropical Storm Pilar Kills at Least 1 in El Salvador
2023-11-01.abstract: The storm was drifting north toward the Central American country early Wednesday. It was forecast to begin moving west, farther into the Pacific Ocean, later in the day.

2023-11-01.abstract: At a U.K. summit, 28 governments, including China and the U.S., signed a declaration agreeing to cooperate on evaluating the risks of artificial intelligence.

2023-11-02.abstract: Hundreds of experienced fighters have joined Kyiv’s ranks to improve their financial fortunes, reflecting the recruitment struggles faced by both sides of the conflict.

2023-11-02.title: China’s Male Leaders Signal to Women That Their Place Is in the Home
2023-11-02.abstract: The Communist Party’s solution to the country’s demographic crisis and a slowing economy is to push women back into traditional roles.

2023-11-02.title: Woman Charged With Murder in Suspected Mushroom Poisoning Case
2023-11-02.abstract: In a case that has gripped Australia, three people fell ill and died soon after eating lunch at the home of a relative.

2023-11-02.title: Kenyan Runners Set the World’s Pace, Chased by a Doping Crisis
2023-11-02.abstract: Nearly 300 athletes from Kenya have been punished for using banned substances since 2015, tarnishing the country’s image as a running powerhouse.

2023-11-02.title: ‘More Than Just Rugby’: Championship Generates Harmony in South Africa
2023-11-02.abstract: South Africans are savoring a second consecutive World Cup victory, producing a racial unity that even Hollywood couldn’t make up and an escape from the country’s troubles.

2023-11-03.title: As Cars Have Grown Bigger, Parking Spaces Might, Too
2023-11-03.abstract: Australians are grumbling about a recent invasion of American-style giant pickup trucks. But even before that, vehicle sizes were increasing.

2023-11-03.abstract: Even as the war’s devastation rages on, Ukrainians have found space to mourn an actor who brought them comfort and laughter.

2023-11-03.abstract: Lena Beseiso, a U.S. citizen, described her journey from Gaza to Egypt.

2023-11-03.abstract: A local official reportedly said the center’s manager had been arrested, and he blamed overcrowding at the facility for the high toll.

2023-11-04.title: Hamas put wounded fighters on departure lists, delaying efforts to evacuate foreigners, a U.S. official says.
2023-11-04.abstract: People holding dual, U.S. or other foreign citizenship had been unable to cross the Gaza border into Egypt for weeks.

2023-11-04.title: Israel Used 2,000-Pound Bombs in Strike on Jabaliya, Analysis Shows
2023-11-04.abstract: Impact craters from the Oct. 31 strike on the densely packed Gaza neighborhood are approximately 40 feet wide. Israel said it was aiming at underground Hamas targets.

2023-11-04.title: Why China and Australia Are Reconciling. Sort of.
2023-11-04.abstract: Both sides have been cautious in the run-up to Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s trip to Beijing, avoiding terms like “reset” in favor of “stabilization.”

2023-11-04.abstract: Representative Tlaib has been on the leading edge of a group of Democrats critical of the president’s embrace of Israel, but the accusation marks an extraordinary broadside from a lawmaker of his own party.

2023-11-04.title: ‘Just Like Medicine’: A New Push for Divorce in a Nation Where It’s Illegal
2023-11-04.abstract: A campaign in the Philippines that frames divorce as a basic human right is gaining momentum, despite systemic and religious barriers.

2023-11-05.title: ‘I Am Dreaming It Will Stop’: A Deadlocked War Tests Ukrainian Morale
2023-11-05.abstract: Pessimism over prospects for a quick victory is increasing, and the spirit that infused the first days of the conflict is starting to fade, polls find.

2023-11-05.title: Tens of thousands rallied in the U.S. in support of Palestinians.
2023-11-05.abstract: In Cincinnati, hundreds clogged a neighborhood once dominated by German immigrants, chanting pro-Palestinian slogans under the watchful eye of police. In San Francisco, thousands filled Civic Center Plaza, many waving Palestinian flags and leaving mementos at an altar to honor those killed in the war in Gaza.

2023-11-05.title: French Nuns and Climate Activists Square Off Over Plans for a Megachurch
2023-11-05.abstract: A yearslong standoff over an enormous church complex in a natural park recently broke out in clashes, when the nuns gave chase.

2023-11-05.title: Among Hamas Hostages: More Than 20 Thais, Half a World From Home
2023-11-05.abstract: Dozens of farm workers from Thailand were kidnapped or killed in the raids on Israel, and relatives want answers. “We have nothing to do with their war,” one said.

2023-11-06.abstract: The U.S. secretary of state made two unannounced visits on Sunday, the first to the Israeli-occupied West Bank and the second to Baghdad.

2023-11-06.abstract: Afghanistan’s cricket team has won big games and many fans in an international competition, in a stark contrast to the pariah status of its government.

2023-11-06.abstract: Since Russian troops were pushed out of the city of Kherson, they have bombed it relentlessly. Closely held river operations could change that.

2023-11-06.abstract: Doctors say they are performing surgeries without anesthesia after weeks of Israeli bombings and siege left severe shortages of medicine, water, food and fuel. “We choose who gets ventilation by deciding who has the best chance of survival,” one doctor said.

2023-11-06.title: Called to Serve, Israeli Reservists Wait to Deploy
2023-11-06.abstract: Facing a military campaign with no end in sight, tens of thousands of soldiers in Israel find themselves in a holding pattern.

2023-11-07.title: Gaza Death Toll Has Hit 10,000, Its Health Ministry Says
2023-11-07.abstract: The figures could not be independently verified, but a Pentagon spokesman acknowledged on Monday that “we know the numbers are in the thousands.”

2023-11-07.abstract: Israel’s military surrounded Gaza City as the enclave plunged into another communications blackout, sowing panic among relatives fearful of the fate of loved ones who remain there.

2023-11-07.abstract: The Brazilian government has raided neo-Nazi groups across 10 states this year, part of a push by the new Lula administration to prosecute far-right extremists.

2023-11-07.abstract: In keeping with tradition, he outlined the priorities of the prime minister at the opening of Parliament — including, this year, more fossil fuel extraction.

2023-11-08.abstract: The Hungarian leader’s efforts have been undermined by setbacks for some of his political allies across Europe and deep divisions over the war in Ukraine.

2023-11-08.abstract: With so much in the war against Russia hinging on refilling the ranks of soldiers, efforts are underway to draw more Ukrainian women into the army.

2023-11-08.abstract: The proposed change, reportedly at the behest of parents and employees who found her story difficult to explain to children, comes amid acute concern about rising antisemitism in Germany.

2023-11-08.abstract: Costa Rica and Panama are busing people through their countries to ease the concentration of people along their borders, which Biden officials fear will encourage more people to travel to the U.S. border.

2023-11-08.abstract: Our chief Africa reporter was wary of journalism that made broad predictions about the continent, but a statistic made clear that staggering change was approaching.

2023-11-09.abstract: To U.S. allies in Asia, the sudden focus on Gaza risks progress on America’s long-delayed pivot to the Indo-Pacific region.

2023-11-09.abstract: Trapped in the enclave, Palestinians on the social media platform are documenting and sharing their harrowing experiences of life under Israeli airstrikes and a ground invasion.

2023-11-10.abstract: A surprising tale of Northern Territory chutzpah.

2023-11-10.abstract: He calls fake news an enemy that threatens democracy. Critics of President Yoon Suk Yeol say he is silencing journalists in the name of fighting disinformation.

2023-11-10.abstract: A government campaign against what it calls “homosexual propaganda” is unsettling booksellers and has resulted in the firing of the director of one of the country’s leading museums.

2023-11-10.abstract: A push from the U.S. for Israel to curtail its bombardment of Gaza.

2023-11-10.abstract: Officials blame immigrants and liberal housing laws, but a Times investigation found the entrenched problems that turned downtown Johannesburg into a blighted tinderbox.

2023-11-11.abstract: The gathering drew an estimated 300,000 people, the police said, making it one of the largest protests in Britain in recent years. Before it began, far-right demonstrators scuffled with officers.

2023-11-11.abstract: After multiple maritime clashes, the Philippines invited journalists on a mission to resupply the Sierra Madre. A reporter for The Times was given rare access.

2023-11-12.abstract: Demonstrators marched in support of Palestine and demanded a cease-fire in Gaza in one of Britain’s largest protests in recent years.

2023-11-12.abstract: First concocted a century ago, the spread is widely adored by Australians — and loathed by almost everyone else.

2023-11-12.abstract: As bloodshed surges around them, an Israeli settler and a Palestinian tour guide search for answers — and a shred of hope.

2023-11-13.abstract: The National Unity Government of Myanmar, formed as an alternative to the junta that orchestrated a 2021 coup, has to battle global apathy and ignorance as it struggles for recognition.

2023-11-13.abstract: Though she was never convicted, Ms. de Lima was detained for six years after she fiercely opposed the Philippine former president’s brutal war on drugs.

2023-11-14.abstract: After the Oct. 7 terror attack, Israel banned Gazan farm workers, and many other foreign laborers returned to their home countries.

2023-11-15.abstract: Video released by Gaza’s Health Ministry shows more than a dozen newborn babies being transferred to patient beds at Al-Shifa hospital, after the hospital’s incubators lost power.

2023-11-15.abstract: FIFA’s president, Gianni Infantino, cheered a plan to take soccer’s richest event to the kingdom. He has said little about his years of work to make that happen.

2023-11-16.abstract: A raid of a hospital that Israel asserts is a base for Hamas is shaping up as a watershed moment in a conflict that has put the Mideast on edge.

2023-11-16.abstract: President Biden emphasized that the Israeli-Palestinian conflict will not end without a two-state solution.

2023-11-16.abstract: A younger generation of Israeli and Palestinian peacemakers want to be part of the dialogue about the “day after” the war, when Israelis and Palestinians must grapple again with how to live side by side.

2023-11-16.abstract: Seven months into Sudan’s civil war, the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces and its allies are ransacking and capturing the Darfur region. An aid worker said, “People are dying like insects.”

2023-11-16.abstract: After weeks of division, the Council adopted a resolution that stopped short of calling for a cease-fire.

2023-11-17.abstract: Human rights experts have estimated that some 14,000 people died from torture or were killed in the prisons run by military intelligence and security forces during Syria’s long civil war.

2023-11-17.abstract: Sally Abed, a Palestinian citizen of Israel, and Alon-Lee Green, a Jewish Israeli, found polarization in America over the war in Gaza. They also found longing for a new approach.

2023-11-17.abstract: While the conflict over the breakaway region of Nagorno-Karabakh concluded with an Azerbaijani victory, resentments simmer on both sides, creating fertile ground for new violence.

2023-11-17.title: Friday Briefing
2023-11-17.abstract: Israel’s search of a major hospital in Gaza.

2023-11-17.abstract: Chuwit Kamolvisit has enthralled Thailand for decades with revelations of police and political corruption. But his own compromised past, including time as a “super pimp,” clouds his legacy.

2023-11-18.abstract: President George Weah, a former soccer star whose administration had weathered accusations of corruption, announced that he had lost his bid for re-election to Joseph Boakai, a 78-year-old former vice president.

2023-11-18.title: Satellite imagery shows a water plant in Gaza City burned down amid a dire water shortage.
2023-11-18.abstract: It was not clear what caused the fire, or how recently the plant had been operating.

2023-11-18.abstract: The Battle of Tarawa, in the middle of the Pacific Ocean, took a heavy toll on American forces and led to outrage at home.

2023-11-18.abstract: The 6.7-magnitude earthquake struck near General Santos City, where hundreds were injured, and schools and shopping malls were closed.

2023-11-18.abstract: With the contentious Rwanda plan in disarray, experts say the U.K. government should focus on a huge backlog of asylum cases, including 50,000 people in hotels.

2023-11-19.title: In Hitler’s Birthplace, Soul-Searching Over a Poisonous Past
2023-11-19.abstract: The Austrian government is turning the house where Hitler was born into a police station. But many think it should be used instead to teach essential lessons about history.

2023-11-19.abstract: Experts say the government should preserve cultural and architectural heritage, while also building disaster-resistant homes. In the meantime, residents are living in tents and in limbo.

2023-11-19.abstract: While most of Europe’s leagues engage in a Sisyphean quest to source as much money as possible, Sweden has chosen a different model. But its rewards come with risk.

2023-11-19.abstract: Every morning, Ahmed Mansour, a Palestinian filmmaker in the U.S., texts his family in Gaza to check if they are still alive. Israel’s siege of the territory has caused widespread communication blackouts, making it difficult for him to reach his parents and siblings.

2023-11-19.abstract: Since 2006, Gershon Baskin, an Israeli peace activist, and Ghazi Hamad, a Hamas official, maintained a secret back channel between Gaza and Israel. Then Oct. 7 happened.

2023-11-20.abstract: A dominant World Cup run closes with a loss to Australia in the final, a symbol of how far India has come and how far it still has to go.

2023-11-20.abstract: Thousands of fans had already traveled to the stadium in Rio de Janeiro by the time the show on Saturday was called off because of safety concerns.

2023-11-20.title: Between Israelis and Palestinians, a Lethal Psychological Chasm Grows
2023-11-20.abstract: In a conflict marked by complete incomprehension on both sides, the ability to see each other as human has been lost.

2023-11-20.abstract: Ukraine is facing continual eastern assaults from Russian forces at a bloody cost for both sides, even as the lines on the map barely move.

2023-11-20.abstract: Prosecutors had charged the pop star with failing to pay 14.5 million euros in income taxes. Her trial had been set to begin on Monday.

2023-11-21.abstract: Planes were built to trust GPS signals. Jamming and spoofing in the Middle East and Ukraine have diverted flights and caused inaccurate onboard alerts.

2023-11-21.abstract: Prime Minister Rishi Sunak is 20 percentage points behind in opinion polls. But history suggests the timing of a vote might make a difference.

2023-11-21.abstract: Children with conditions like ADHD and autism have been particularly affected by the traumas and uncertainties of the war and the disruptions to daily life, families and experts say.

2023-11-21.abstract: Many of the more than 200 people seized by Hamas when it raided Israel had serious medical conditions. Some were badly injured in the attack. Doctors say they need medical care urgently.

2023-11-21.abstract: Injured patients, including children, were moved by bus from one Gaza hospital to another on Monday.

2023-11-22.abstract: The agreement calls for a pause in the fighting and for Hamas to free 50 of the captives it seized in its Oct. 7 raid on Israel. Hamas said Israel would release 150 Palestinians held in Israeli prisons.

2023-11-22.abstract: Unusually, a protest vote may be coalescing around a centrist, Pieter Omtzigt, as the Dutch vote in national elections on Wednesday.

2023-11-22.abstract: Facilities remain hobbled by Russian attacks, repair work is unfinished and there is little spare equipment, although air defenses have been strengthened.

2023-11-22.abstract: Yaël Braun-Pivet, a secular Jew, cherishes the French ideal of a national identity that subsumes ethnic division — and speaks out when her country falls short.

2023-11-23.abstract: Israeli and American officials have said that Hamas uses hospitals for cover. Hamas and administrators of Al-Shifa, Gaza’s largest hospital, have denied the claims.

2023-11-23.abstract: Biden administration officials are watching whether the British government’s attempt to salvage its Rwanda asylum policy risks undermining the Northern Ireland peace accord.

2023-11-23.abstract: Husbands and wives, parents and children, wonder when, or if, they will ever see each other again.

2023-11-23.title: Chinese Hospital Overloaded as Child Respiratory Illnesses Surge
2023-11-23.abstract: Families crowded the waiting room and registration area of Capital Institute of Pediatrics, a hospital in Beijing, with respiratory illnesses in children increasing in the country.

2023-11-23.abstract: Reports of overcrowding at pediatric hospitals in China have raised concerns about a jump in respiratory illnesses affecting children.

2023-11-24.abstract: Prodded by the U.S., Israel agreed to the pause in hostilities and the release of 150 imprisoned Palestinians in exchange for 50 hostages held in Gaza.

2023-11-24.title: W.H.O. Says China Has Shared Data Indicating No Novel Pathogen
2023-11-24.abstract: The W.H.O. had requested detailed information about a reported surge in respiratory illnesses in children in China. Chinese data suggested the surge was caused by known bacteria and viruses.

2023-11-24.title: How a North Korean Soccer Prodigy Vanished, and Re-emerged
2023-11-24.abstract: Han Kwang-song’s recent appearances in World Cup qualifiers were his first ones overseas since 2020, when U.N. sanctions led to an involuntary career break.

2023-11-24.title: Firefighters Battle Blazes in Australia
2023-11-24.abstract: Helicopters help douse flames in Perth as Western Australia deals with a heat wave.

2023-11-25.title: In a Poignant Moment, Israelis Welcome Some Hostages Home
2023-11-25.abstract: The return of 13 Israeli hostages out of more than 200 provided a glimmer of hope for a nation still traumatized by the Hamas assault of Oct. 7.

2023-11-25.title: First Captives Freed in Tense Gaza Truce Between Israel and Hamas
2023-11-25.abstract: Hamas freed two dozen hostages, Israel released 39 imprisoned Palestinians, and more than 130 aid trucks reached Gaza over the first day of a tense, temporary truce after seven weeks of war.

2023-11-25.title: Waiting and Worrying Continue for Families of American Hostages
2023-11-25.abstract: No Americans were released in the first day of the temporary cease-fire, but officials and families were hopeful they would be freed in the coming days.

2023-11-25.title: Surviving Winter in a Decimated Ukrainian Village
2023-11-25.abstract: Winter in Ukraine’s eastern steppe brings an inescapable cold. The wind blowing through damaged homes, the shattered windows, the chill in your bones — it feels as if it will be permanent.

2023-11-25.title: Cockroaches and Mountains of Trash Plague Acapulco After Hurricane
2023-11-25.abstract: Residents complain of rashes and stomach ailments as 666,000 tons of garbage overwhelm the city. Uncollected waste after natural disasters can lead to illnesses, experts said.

2023-11-26.title: Fire Season in Australia Starts, Early and Ominous
2023-11-26.abstract: Though experts do not think that this season will be the worst yet, they also warn that the past is no longer a reliable guide to the future.

2023-11-26.title: The pause in fighting enters a third day.
2023-11-26.abstract: Halfway through a four-day pause in fighting that so far has brought two swaps of hostages and prisoners, Israeli leaders faced a dilemma over whether to restart the war against Hamas in the Gaza Strip once the truce ends on Tuesday morning.

2023-11-26.title: A Thai mother rejoices at learning her son is alive.
2023-11-26.abstract: Watsana Yojampa spoke to her son after he was released by Hamas. There are at least 18 Thai citizens still being held hostage in the Gaza Strip.

2023-11-26.title: Need Therapy? In West Africa, Hairdressers Can Help.
2023-11-26.abstract: An initiative to train hairdressers in mental health counseling is providing relief to hundreds of clients in a region with the world’s least access to therapy.

2023-11-26.title: Battle for Influence Rages in Heart of Wagner’s Operations in Africa
2023-11-26.abstract: The death of the mercenary group’s leader has created a window of opportunity in the Central African Republic for Western powers to offer an alternative.

2023-11-27.title: U.S. Navy Rescues Ship From Somali Pirate Attack
2023-11-27.abstract: The United States says the episode is unrelated to regional tensions, though it is still investigating the firing of two missiles at the Navy ship involved in the rescue.

2023-11-27.title: Female Rangers ‘Don’t Go All Alpha Like the Men’ to Protect a Forest
2023-11-27.abstract: Rather than take a confrontational approach with trespassers looking to farm or log in a tropical rainforest in Indonesia, teams of women rangers try dialogue first.

2023-11-27.title: Trapped in a Tunnel for 15 Days, With No End in Sight
2023-11-27.abstract: The effort to rescue 41 construction workers in India has faced repeated setbacks. Now the authorities are trying to drill vertically through a mountain.

2023-11-27.title: Russian Women Protest Long Deployments for Soldiers in Ukraine
2023-11-27.abstract: “Make way for someone else,” a new grass-roots movement demands as women challenge the official argument that the mobilized troops are needed in combat indefinitely.

2023-11-27.title: Storm Batters Southern Ukraine, Spreading Misery for Civilians and Soldiers
2023-11-27.abstract: Violent waves stirred by hurricane-force winds threatened to tear maritime mines from their moorings in the Sea of Azov and the Black Sea.

2023-11-28.title: In New Zealand’s Crackdown on Crime, What Part Can Maori Wardens Play?
2023-11-28.abstract: The strategies used by the Indigenous community policing alternative are in stark contrast to more muscular tactics pitched by the incoming government.

2023-11-28.title: New Zealand’s New Government Says It Will Scrap Smoking Ban
2023-11-28.abstract: The law, celebrated as a model for other countries, would have eventually made tobacco illegal.

2023-11-28.title: Americans Love Avocados. It’s Killing Mexico’s Forests.
2023-11-28.abstract: Illegal deforestation for avocado crops points to a blood-soaked trade with the United States involving threats, abductions and killings.

2023-11-28.title: Vietnam Relied on Environmentalists to Secure Billions. Then It Jailed Them.
2023-11-28.abstract: The government is preparing to present its energy transition plan at the U.N. climate talks as it intensifies a crackdown on environmental advocates.

2023-11-28.title: Can U.S.-China Student Exchanges Survive Geopolitics?
2023-11-28.abstract: The flow of students between the countries has been a mainstay of their relationship, even when ties have soured. Now these exchanges, too, are under threat.

2023-11-29.title: Hamas Releases 12 More Hostages as Nations’ Spy Chiefs Go to Qatar for Talks
2023-11-29.abstract: Mediators hope the current model of a daily cease-fire with the release of Israeli captives in exchange for freeing Palestinian prisoners would create the conditions for longer-term discussions.

2023-11-29.title: Kenya’s Leader Lifts His Global Profile. At Home, the Public Fumes.
2023-11-29.abstract: President William Ruto has drawn attention internationally as a climate champion and peace mediator but faces domestic discontent over rising food and fuel prices, and new taxes.

2023-11-29.title: The truce between Israel and Hamas held for another day.
2023-11-29.abstract: Hours after Hamas and Israel completed the fifth exchange of hostages and prisoners, their fragile truce continued into Wednesday, allowing for more people to be released on both sides, as global leaders called for a longer pause in fighting.

2023-11-29.title: At Least One Dead as U.S. Osprey Crashes in Japan With 8 Onboard
2023-11-29.abstract: The incident comes three months after three Marines died in an Osprey crash in Australia.

2023-11-29.title: Germany’s Much-Vaunted Strategic Pivot Stalls
2023-11-29.abstract: In the days after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Chancellor Olaf Scholz promised to revitalize Germany’s military. Nearly two years on, major change has yet to be felt.

2023-11-30.title: Here’s What We Know About the Hostages Released on Wednesday
2023-11-30.abstract: The sixth round of hostage releases since the cease-fire began involved 10 Israelis, four Thai nationals and two Israeli-Russian dual citizens.

2023-11-30.title: The activist Ahed Tamimi is among the Palestinians freed in the latest exchange.
2023-11-30.abstract: The 22-year-old activist was jailed for three weeks without being charged. Her arrest was one of thousands made during the Israeli military’s crackdown in the occupied West Bank after the Hamas attacks on Oct. 7.

2023-11-30.title: Russia Declares Gay Rights Movement as ‘Extremist’
2023-11-30.abstract: Activists said the designation could put L.G.B.T.Q. people and their organizations under threat of criminal prosecution for something as simple as displaying the rainbow flag.

2023-11-30.title: Shooting in Jerusalem Kills at Least 3 People, Israeli Officials Say
2023-11-30.abstract: Israel’s Shin Bet security service said the gunmen were Palestinian brothers from East Jerusalem who were affiliated with Hamas.

2023-11-30.title: ‘They Came in the Dark’: Settler Violence Intensifies in the West Bank
2023-11-30.abstract: Since the Oct. 7 attacks in Israel, extremist settlers in the West Bank have been emboldened, displacing more than 1,000 Palestinians, according to the United Nations.
